# AI-generated file descriptions for smart search — design

> **What is this file.** Implementation contract for adding locally-generated,
> AI-authored file descriptions to the smart-search results.
> **Audience:** engineers implementing the feature, and reviewers.
> **Owner:** marcello.barile.
> **Companion files:** the implementation plan (to be created via the
> writing-plans workflow under `docs/superpowers/plans/`) sequences the work;
> this spec is the contract. Prior context: smart-search was built in
> `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-27-smart-search-design.md`.

## Status legend

- **Shipped / Delivered** — merged to the production codebase, available to users.
- **PoC implemented (not delivered)** — prototype only.
- **Planned** — designed here, not yet implemented.

Everything in this document is **Planned** unless stated otherwise.

## Problem

Smart-search stores a short `description` per indexed file, shown in results.
Today it is heuristic: it prefers a genuine file-header comment, else a
structural summary (`Defines X.` / `Exports A, b.`). For many files this is not
informative — a class name or export list does not explain what the file *does*.
The earlier comment-scraping approach was worse: it captured comments attached to
an import or the first declaration, producing misleading descriptions.

We want a short natural-language description of the file's purpose, generated by
a small language model that runs fully offline (no network, no dependency on the
bundled `claude` CLI).

## Goals

- One-sentence, whole-file description generated by a local model.
- Fully offline; no new native dependency.
- No cost added to indexing time (thousands of files).
- Graceful degradation: heuristic description always available as fallback.
- User can turn the feature off.

## Non-goals

- Summarizing the entire workspace up front.
- Multi-sentence documentation or symbol-level docs.
- Re-using the `claude` CLI or any network model.

## Key constraint that shapes the design

Embeddings already run through `@xenova/transformers` (transformers.js over the
Open Neural Network Exchange (ONNX) Runtime), loaded offline from `resources/models` (see
`packages/theia-extensions/src/node/search/embedding-model.ts`). The same runtime
runs **text generation** with small instruct models in ONNX format. Therefore we
reuse it — **no `node-llama-cpp`, no native module, no electron-builder rebuild
changes.** Only a second ONNX model is vendored, the same way the embedding model
already is (`scripts/fetch-search-model.mjs` →
electron-builder globs `resources/models/**`).

## Decisions (resolved during brainstorming)

| Decision | Choice |
|---|---|
| Model | `Qwen2.5-Coder-1.5B-Instruct` (ONNX, quantized). Code-aware; ~1 GB installer add. |
| When to generate | Lazy, on-demand for files shown in results; cached. Never at index time. |
| Cache key | File content hash (already stored per index record). |
| Fallback while generating / on failure | Show the heuristic description immediately; swap in the AI text when ready. |
| Feature toggle | Preference `spexr.search.aiDescriptions.enabled`, boolean, default `true`. |

## Architecture

### Backend (node)

**`description-generator.ts`** (new) — wraps
`pipeline("text-generation", "Qwen2.5-Coder-1.5B-Instruct", { quantized: true })`.

- Offline: `env.allowRemoteModels = false`, `env.localModelPath = resolveModelsDir()`
  (the resolver is shared with the embedder; extract it to a small shared module
  so both consumers use one implementation).
- Lazy load on first `generate()` call.
- Public surface:
  - `generate(relPath: string, content: string): Promise<string | null>` — one
    sentence describing the file; `null` if the model is unavailable.
  - `isAvailable(): boolean` — false once a load attempt has failed, so callers
    stop requesting.
- **Serialized queue:** one pipeline, one generation at a time. Requests are
  processed first-in-first-out (FIFO). In-flight requests are de-duplicated by `relPath` so concurrent
  asks for the same file share one generation.
- Load failure (model missing / runtime error): mark unavailable, resolve all
  pending and future requests to `null`. No infinite retry.

**Prompt / decoding:**
- Input: `relPath` + the first ~1500 characters of content.
- Instruction: produce a single sentence (≤ ~120 chars) describing what the file
  does, no preamble.
- Decoding: `max_new_tokens ≈ 40`, greedy, stop on newline; trim to one sentence
  and cap at the existing `MAX_DESC_CHARS` (120).

**`SpexrSearchBackendService`** — new RPC method:
- `describeFile(root: string, path: string): Promise<string | null>`
  1. Resolve the workspace + index record for `path`.
  2. If the record already has `aiDescription` for the current hash → return it.
  3. Else, read the file, call `generator.generate(path, content)`. On a non-null
     result, store it on the record (`aiDescription`), `save()` the index, return it.
  4. Return `null` if the record is missing, the model is unavailable, or
     generation fails.

### Protocol

`search-protocol.ts` (the remote procedure call (RPC) contract between frontend and backend):
- Add `describeFile(root, path): Promise<string | null>` to `SpexrSearchService`.
- `SearchHit` is unchanged — the heuristic `description` keeps flowing as today.

### Index

`vector-index.ts`:
- `IndexRecord` gains `aiDescription?: string`, persisted in the serialized index.
- Invalidation is implicit: a content change produces a new record (new hash)
  without `aiDescription`, so the next `describeFile` regenerates.
- `reindex` (force rebuild) drops `aiDescription` along with the rest; it is
  re-populated lazily afterward.

### Frontend (`smart-search-widget.tsx`)

- Reads the preference `spexr.search.aiDescriptions.enabled` via `PreferenceService`.
- For each **visible** result (top-N shown), if the feature is on and the hit has
  no AI description yet: render the heuristic `description` immediately, prefixed
  by a **pulsing AI icon**, and call `describeFile(root, path)`.
- On resolve with text: swap the description text, stop the pulse (icon becomes
  **solid**), cache the value on the in-memory hit so re-renders don't re-request.
- On resolve with `null` (unavailable / failed): keep the heuristic text, **no icon**.
- When the preference is off: heuristic only, never call `describeFile`, no icon.
- Requests are limited to currently-rendered results (no fan-out over the full
  result set) and de-duplicated per path on the frontend too.

### Icon states (UX)

| State | Icon | Tooltip (hover) |
|---|---|---|
| Generating | AI icon, pulsing | "L'AI sta generando una descrizione del file…" |
| Ready (AI text shown) | AI icon, solid | "Descrizione generata dall'AI" |
| Unavailable / failed / disabled | none | — |

(Output language of the tooltip follows the product UI locale via the existing
native language support (nls) mechanism; the strings above are the source
English/Italian intent.)

### Preference

`spexr-preferences.ts`:
- Add `SPEXR_SEARCH_AI_DESCRIPTIONS_PREFERENCE = "spexr.search.aiDescriptions.enabled"`.
- Schema: `{ type: "boolean", default: true, description: "Generate AI file
  descriptions locally for search results. Turn off to skip the local model and
  show heuristic descriptions only." }`.

### Packaging

- Extend `scripts/fetch-search-model.mjs` to also fetch the generation model into
  `resources/models` (a `text-generation` pipeline warm-up call, mirroring the
  embedding fetch).
- electron-builder already includes `resources/models/**` (extraFiles +
  asarUnpack), so no electron-builder config change is required.
- Installer grows by ~1 GB. Documented as an accepted trade-off.

## Data flow

1. Query → backend returns hits with the heuristic `description` (unchanged).
2. Widget renders results; for visible hits without an AI description and with the
   feature enabled, calls `describeFile`.
3. Backend: cache-hit by hash → immediate return. Cache-miss → enqueue generation,
   persist `aiDescription`, return.
4. Widget swaps text and flips the icon from pulsing to solid.
5. Indexing / reindex never trigger generation; it stays lazy.

## Error handling

- Model absent or load failure → `describeFile` returns `null`; UI keeps the
  heuristic, no icon, no retry storm (`isAvailable()` gate).
- Single-file generation failure → `null`; heuristic remains.
- All generation is in-process and serialized, so CPU is not saturated by
  concurrent inference.

## Testing

- `description-generator`: with a fake pipeline — serialization (one at a time),
  in-flight de-duplication by path, and `null` fallback after a simulated load
  failure (and that it stays unavailable without retrying).
- `describeFile`: cache-hit by hash returns without generating; cache-miss
  generates, persists `aiDescription`, and returns; returns `null` when the model
  is unavailable or the record is missing.
- `vector-index`: `aiDescription` round-trips through serialize/deserialize; a
  content change (new hash) drops it.
- Widget: icon state rendering (pulsing / solid / absent) and the heuristic→AI
  text swap; no `describeFile` call when the preference is off.

## Rollback

Self-contained and additive. Reverting the feature is removing the new module,
the RPC method, the optional record field, the preference, and the widget
changes; the heuristic description path is untouched and remains the default
behavior.
